title: Network connection timing out inside podman container
body:
### Issue Description

In a podman container, we are facing connection timing out when connecting to a socket. The code which runs inside container is similar to this
```
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import socket
import time

server='142.251.46.174'
port=80

conn = 1
while True:
  s = socket.socket()
  s.settimeout(3)
  print( "connecting..." )
  s.connect( (server, port))
  print( "connected." )
  time.sleep(1)
  s.close()
  print( "connection {}".format( conn ) )
  conn += 1
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a container from an image which has the Python script
2. sudo podman exec -it <ctnr> ./script.py

### Describe the results you received

After some iterations we can see the request timing out
```
.
.
connection 227
connecting...
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/root/./conn.py", line 15, in <module>
    s.connect( (server, port))
TimeoutError: timed out
e74d803d9a17:~#
```

### Describe the results you expected

No connection timeout.

comments.body: Did you test this on the host or with `--network host`, it could be very well a problem with you network and not podman.

No, I am using podman network only. You can see the 'podman inspect' output
```
"NetworkSettings": {
               "EndpointID": "",
               "Gateway": "10.88.0.1",
               "IPAddress": "10.88.2.199",
               "IPPrefixLen": 16,
               "IPv6Gateway": "",
               "GlobalIPv6Address": "",
               "GlobalIPv6PrefixLen": 0,
               "MacAddress": "ea:59:69:b9:fa:4c",
               "Bridge": "",
               "SandboxID": "",
               "HairpinMode": false,
               "LinkLocalIPv6Address": "",
               "LinkLocalIPv6PrefixLen": 0,
               "Ports": {},
               "SandboxKey": "/run/netns/netns-d16b17d8-bd62-82a7-2882-a858f60fd885",
               "Networks": {
                    "podman": {
                         "EndpointID": "",
                         "Gateway": "10.88.0.1",
                         "IPAddress": "10.88.2.199",
                         "IPPrefixLen": 16,
                         "IPv6Gateway": "",
                         "GlobalIPv6Address": "",
                         "GlobalIPv6PrefixLen": 0,
                         "MacAddress": "ea:59:69:b9:fa:4c",
                         "NetworkID": "podman",
                         "DriverOpts": null,
                         "IPAMConfig": null,
                         "Links": null,
                         "Aliases": [
                              "5c14d0b09747"
                         ]
                    }
               }
          },
```

On the host the script runs fine with no network connection timing out.
Were are you connecting to? Can you try to connect to an ip on your host, I don't think podman is doing anything special here. Does it work if you increase the timeout?

> Were are you connecting to? 

For my use case, I wanted to connect to an intranet server but this network timeout is happening for local, intranet, WAN connect.
> Can you try to connect to an ip on your host, I don't think podman is doing anything special here. 

I tried, still getting timeout
> Does it work if you increase the timeout?

No, still getting connection timeout for a 10-second timeout.

some more debugging information -

1.The above test loop runs fine, without any connect timeouts when there are no containers being created concurrently.
2.Along with the above test loop, if we run one/more loops where we are just creating and destroying more podman rootful containers then we can very easily see "stalls" during connect and eventually hitting the timeout.
Something like
```
while true
do
  sudo podman run --rm -it nicolaka/netshoot date
done
```
The stall and connect timeout happens pretty much within 5 mins of running the test loop.

The above issue does not happen at all with docker containers. As per the docs, rootful podman containers are wired in the same way as docker containers in terms of iptables and Linux bridge connections. So not sure what difference is causing this.

If we don't run the podman run command loops (2) then the connect timeouts do not happen.

Using tcpdump we have verified that, when the "connect" timesout, we can see the SYN packet as outgoing in the container, reaching podman0 and not going out over the host interface. The "connect" is being done to an intranet service and most of the times the connection succeeds. There is no issue with the intranet service.

We suspect the packet is getting delayed in the host stack but not sure why this issue happens only sometimes. 

When the connect timeout is unset the connect eventually succeeds.

If the test loop is run on the host then there is no connect timeout. We suspect something during "podman run" is slowing down the "connect" calls in the test loop container. Some contention with iptables/conntrack etc?

Note that "ping" from inside the container to the intranet host with the service runs without any issues. It is only the TCP traffic that seems be impacted.

Thanks @kthommandra, that is useful information.

When you create/destroy other containers concurrently do they run in a different network? This could be similar to https://github.com/containers/netavark/issues/210.

Can you reproduce this with a recent kernel? This could be some kernel bug. I am not sure why packages would be dropped.

All the create/destroy podman containers are being done in the same network podman0.
The kernel version we used was 5.10.62

We are not running anything on this server, its a fresh server with only podman running.

### To reproduce the error

1. In one of the terminal create a container with this `sudo podman run --rm -it nicolaka/netshoot bash`
1.1.  Copy and run this script inside this container
```
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import socket
import time

server='142.251.46.174'
port=80

conn = 1
while True:
  s = socket.socket()
  s.settimeout(3)
  print( "connecting..." )
  s.connect( (server, port))
  print( "connected." )
  time.sleep(1)
  s.close()
  print( "connection {}".format( conn ) )
  conn += 1
```

2. In the second terminal run this
```
while true
do
  sudo podman run --rm -it nicolaka/netshoot date
done
```

3. Error received: Within 5 mins you would see this error for the python script you ran inside the container
```
connection 6
connecting...
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/root/script.py", line 13, in <module>
    s.connect( (server, port))
TimeoutError: timed out
```

I also ran into this issue and had to analyze and fix it to satisfy a customer of ours.

I was able to reproduce this issue under the following circumstances using only one host:
- You use Enterprise Linux (Alma, Rocky, RHEL)
- You have two bridge networks, net0 and net1 (name arbitrary)
- You have one container on net0 which publishes ports
- You have a second container (host does also work) on net0 and net1
- The issue occurs for any outbound communication that leaves on the network interface corresponding to net0 (this is a random component - sometime the packets leave on one interface, sometimes on the other one!)

You can see the whole thing in action at https://github.com/Galaxy102/podman-el-network-issue-showcase

The solution is to set the `sysctl` `net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter` to `2` before creating the podman networks. Perhaps this could be added to the documentation?

> The solution is to set the sysctl net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter to 2

this is fixed in https://github.com/containers/netavark/pull/962

But I don't see how this is related to the original report here as it doesn't mention port forwarding at all.
